findings.title: Hardcoded Secret in `password`
findings.detail: Credential stored in source code is exposed to anyone with repo access.
findings.fix: Use environment variables: os.environ.get('SECRET_KEY') or a secrets vault.

findings.title: Hardcoded Secret in `api_key`
findings.detail: Credential stored in source code is exposed to anyone with repo access.
findings.fix: Use environment variables: os.environ.get('SECRET_KEY') or a secrets vault.

findings.title: Hardcoded Secret in `secret_key`
findings.detail: Credential stored in source code is exposed to anyone with repo access.
findings.fix: Use environment variables: os.environ.get('SECRET_KEY') or a secrets vault.

findings.title: Hardcoded Secret in `auth_token`
findings.detail: Credential stored in source code is exposed to anyone with repo access.
findings.fix: Use environment variables: os.environ.get('SECRET_KEY') or a secrets vault.

findings.title: SQL Injection
findings.detail: SQL query built with string formatting — attacker can inject arbitrary SQL.
findings.fix: Use parameterised queries: cursor.execute("SELECT * FROM t WHERE id=?", (val,))

findings.title: SQL Injection
findings.detail: SQL query built with string formatting — attacker can inject arbitrary SQL.
findings.fix: Use parameterised queries: cursor.execute("SELECT * FROM t WHERE id=?", (val,))

findings.title: Code Injection via eval()
findings.detail: eval() executes arbitrary code — dangerous with any user-controlled input.
findings.fix: Remove eval/exec. Use ast.literal_eval() for safe expression parsing.

findings.title: Code Injection via exec()
findings.detail: exec() executes arbitrary code — dangerous with any user-controlled input.
findings.fix: Remove eval/exec. Use ast.literal_eval() for safe expression parsing.

findings.title: Insecure Deserialization (pickle)
findings.detail: pickle.loads() on untrusted data allows arbitrary code execution.
findings.fix: Use JSON or a safe format. Never deserialise untrusted data with pickle.

findings.title: Command Injection via os.system()
findings.detail: os.system() passes a string to the shell — attackable with shell metacharacters.
findings.fix: Use subprocess.run(['cmd', arg], shell=False) with a list of arguments.

findings.title: Command Injection via subprocess shell=True
findings.detail: shell=True passes command to /bin/sh — enables injection if input is user-controlled.
findings.fix: Pass arguments as a list and use shell=False (the default).

findings.title: Weak Hash Algorithm (hashlib.md5)
findings.detail: MD5 and SHA-1 are cryptographically broken and vulnerable to collision attacks.
findings.fix: Use hashlib.sha256() or hashlib.sha3_256() instead.

findings.title: Weak Hash Algorithm (hashlib.sha1)
findings.detail: MD5 and SHA-1 are cryptographically broken and vulnerable to collision attacks.
findings.fix: Use hashlib.sha256() or hashlib.sha3_256() instead.

findings.title: SSL Certificate Verification Disabled
findings.detail: verify=False disables TLS checks — enables man-in-the-middle attacks.
findings.fix: Remove verify=False. Pass verify='/path/to/ca-bundle.crt' for custom CAs.

findings.title: SSL Certificate Verification Disabled
findings.detail: verify=False disables TLS checks — enables man-in-the-middle attacks.
findings.fix: Remove verify=False. Pass verify='/path/to/ca-bundle.crt' for custom CAs.

findings.title: XML External Entity (XXE) Injection
findings.detail: Default XML parsers may process external entities — enables file read or SSRF.
findings.fix: Use defusedxml: import defusedxml.ElementTree as ET

findings.title: Path Traversal via open()
findings.detail: open() called with a variable path — attacker may use '../' to access arbitrary files.
findings.fix: Validate with os.path.abspath() and verify the result starts with your base directory.

findings.title: Unsafe YAML Load
findings.detail: yaml.load() without Loader= can execute arbitrary Python via !!python/object tags.
findings.fix: Use yaml.safe_load() or pass Loader=yaml.SafeLoader explicitly.

findings.title: Path Traversal via open()
findings.detail: open() called with a variable path — attacker may use '../' to access arbitrary files.
findings.fix: Validate with os.path.abspath() and verify the result starts with your base directory.

findings.title: Insecure Module Import: telnetlib
findings.detail: telnetlib sends all data including credentials in plaintext.
findings.fix: Use paramiko or asyncssh for encrypted SSH access.

findings.title: Insecure Random Number Generation
findings.detail: random module is not cryptographically secure — output is predictable.
findings.fix: Use secrets.token_hex(), secrets.randbelow(), or secrets.choice() instead.

findings.title: Insecure Random Number Generation
findings.detail: random module is not cryptographically secure — output is predictable.
findings.fix: Use secrets.token_hex(), secrets.randbelow(), or secrets.choice() instead.

findings.title: Insecure Temporary File (mktemp)
findings.detail: tempfile.mktemp() is vulnerable to TOCTOU race conditions.
findings.fix: Use tempfile.mkstemp() or tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile() instead.
